title: Poor performance when joining on text columns when default_collation is set
description:
### What happens?

https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/pull/13958 disabled join filter pushdown when a join is performed over collated columns. However, this is a very common case. For example, when `default_collation` is set, text columns become collated and join filter pushdown no longer applies. We recently ran into this with pg_duckdb where they need to set `default_collation` to `C`: https://github.com/duckdb/pg_duckdb/issues/898

### To Reproduce

```
CREATE TABLE s (a text);
INSERT INTO s SELECT 'val_' || range FROM range(1000000);;
CREATE TABLE t (b text);
INSERT INTO t VALUES ('val_3');
SET default_collation = 'C';
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT * FROM s, t WHERE a = b;
```
performs full scan on `s` (but removing `SET default_collation = 'C';` will trigger join filter pushdown)

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT);
CREATE TABLE orders (user_id INTEGER, product TEXT);
SELECT u.name, o.product FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.name = o.product;
key_bug_features: Join filter pushdown disabled for collated text columns
